# World Cup 2026 — Findings & Projections

*Generated by the engine in `C:\Users\sambo\Desktop\WorldCup2026`. All numbers
are model output from real data — see methodology at the bottom. The 48-team
field/draw is now the **official 2026 final draw** (made 5 Dec 2025, completed by
the March 2026 play-offs) wired into `models/field_2026.py`; the group-stage
results below are simulated over those real groups (`python run.py simulate
20000`). Knockout pairings are still bracket-randomised — wiring the official
R32 seeding map is the remaining structural to-do.*

---

## 1. Variables tracked (everything publicly digitised that helps prediction)

| Category | Specific variables | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Match results | date, teams, score, tournament, venue, neutral flag — **49,378 matches since 1872** | martj42/international_results |
| Knockout tiebreaks | penalty shootout winners (677) | martj42 shootouts |
| Expected goals (**xG**) | shot-level xG → match xG per team | StatsBomb open data |
| Live state | upcoming fixtures, in-play & final scores | TheSportsDB |
| News inflow | headlines auto-tagged by team + flag (**injury / suspension / lineup / form / transfer / manager**) | BBC, Guardian, Sky, ESPN RSS |
| Derived ratings | **Elo**, **attack/defence strength**, home advantage, time-decayed form | computed |

## 2. Derived measures the engine computes
- **International Elo** — tournament-weighted K-factor, home advantage, goal-difference multiplier (49k matches replayed).
- **Dixon-Coles attack & defence** — Poisson goal-rate strengths, MLE-fit with exponential time decay + L2 shrinkage, low-score (tau) correction.
- **Ensemble 1X2** — blended win/draw/loss + expected goals + most-likely scoreline.
- **Monte Carlo title odds** — 20,000 simulated tournaments.

## 5. Official group stage (12 groups × 4) — with advance-to-knockout odds

The **official final draw**. `Adv%` = probability of finishing top-2 **or** as one
of the 8 best third-placed teams (32 of 48 advance, so the bar is low). Teams
listed by Elo within each group; note `Adv%` is driven by *who else is in the
group*, so it isn't always monotonic with Elo (e.g. Australia clears Türkiye in D,
Germany clears Ecuador in E).

```
GROUP A                  Elo   Adv    GROUP B                  Elo   Adv
1 Mexico                1939   83%    1 Switzerland           1954   95%
2 South Korea           1877   79%    2 Canada                1898   91%
3 Czech Republic        1792   61%    3 Bosnia & Herz.        1649   42%
4 South Africa          1658   48%    4 Qatar                 1582   34%

GROUP C                  Elo   Adv    GROUP D                  Elo   Adv
1 Brazil                2050   96%    1 Turkey                1959   68%
2 Morocco               1995   93%    2 Paraguay              1902   63%
3 Scotland              1834   48%    3 Australia             1895   78%
4 Haiti                 1698   24%    4 United States         1848   65%

GROUP E                  Elo   Adv    GROUP F                  Elo   Adv
1 Ecuador               2008   88%    1 Netherlands           2006   86%
2 Germany               2002   95%    2 Japan                 1992   90%
3 Ivory Coast           1791   80%    3 Sweden                1772   39%
4 Curaçao               1596   10%    4 Tunisia               1730   53%

GROUP G                  Elo   Adv    GROUP H                  Elo   Adv
1 Belgium               1942   90%    1 Spain                 2216   98%
2 Iran                  1885   80%    2 Uruguay               1960   85%
3 Egypt                 1810   69%    3 Saudi Arabia          1691   33%
4 New Zealand           1720   31%    4 Cape Verde            1666   38%

GROUP I                  Elo   Adv    GROUP J                  Elo   Adv
1 France                2134   91%    1 Argentina             2173   97%
2 Norway                1972   79%    2 Austria               1889   71%
3 Senegal               1909   76%    3 Algeria               1871   78%
4 Iraq                  1740   24%    4 Jordan                1776   21%

GROUP K                  Elo   Adv    GROUP L                  Elo   Adv
1 Colombia              2044   86%    1 England               2079   96%
2 Portugal              2031   89%    2 Croatia               1961   86%
3 Uzbekistan            1828   44%    3 Panama                1826   40%
4 DR Congo              1766   46%    4 Ghana                 1606   40%
```

Drawn position order (as seeded): **A** Mexico·South Africa·South Korea·Czechia ·
**B** Canada·Bosnia·Qatar·Switzerland · **C** Brazil·Morocco·Haiti·Scotland ·
**D** USA·Paraguay·Australia·Türkiye · **E** Germany·Curaçao·Ivory Coast·Ecuador ·
**F** Netherlands·Japan·Sweden·Tunisia · **G** Belgium·Egypt·Iran·New Zealand ·
**H** Spain·Cape Verde·Saudi Arabia·Uruguay · **I** France·Senegal·Iraq·Norway ·
**J** Argentina·Algeria·Austria·Jordan · **K** Portugal·DR Congo·Uzbekistan·Colombia ·
**L** England·Croatia·Ghana·Panama.

---

## 6. Tournament structure (the 2026 funnel)

```
   48 TEAMS  ──►  12 GROUPS (A–L), round-robin
       │
       ▼
   ROUND OF 32   ◄── 12 group winners + 12 runners-up + 8 best 3rd-placed
       │              (32 of 48 advance)
       ▼
   ROUND OF 16   ── 16 ── single elimination ──┐
       │                                        │ draws settled by a
       ▼                                        │ relative-strength
   QUARTER-FINALS ── 8 ──                       │ "penalty" proxy
       │                                        │
       ▼                                        │
   SEMI-FINALS ── 4 ──                          │
       │                                        │
       ▼                                        │
   FINAL ── 2 ── ◄──────────────────────────────┘
       │
       ▼
   🏆 CHAMPION 2026   →  model favourite: ARGENTINA (22%), then SPAIN (18%)
```

New venues: 16 host cities across USA, Canada & Mexico. First 48-team,
104-match World Cup. Hosts (USA, Canada, Mexico) seeded into pot 1 by convention.

---

## 7. Headline reads
- **Two clear favourites**: Argentina (22%) and Spain (18%) together hold ~40% of
  title probability — they are the only sides above 2150 Elo, and the draw kept
  them on opposite halves of the bracket (FIFA's pathway split), so they can only
  meet in the final.
- **Chasing pack** (Brazil, England, France, Portugal): each 5–8%; any could win,
  none is dominant.
- **Dark horses**: Morocco (5.2%) and Japan (4.6%) both clear Colombia and sit
  ahead of Germany and the Netherlands on current form-weighted ratings.
- **Toughest draw**: Group E is the "group of death" — Ecuador (88%), Germany
  (95%) and Ivory Coast (80%) are all live, so a real contender exits in the
  group; Curaçao (10%) is the field's longest shot. Group K (Colombia + Portugal)
  pits two top-7 sides against each other.
- **xG layer** is wired (StatsBomb) and demonstrated; expanding it across all
  recent internationals is the highest-value next data task. Encoding the official
  R32 seeding map (knockout pairings are still randomised) is the next structural
  upgrade.

*Methodology: Elo (World Football Elo conventions) + Dixon-Coles Poisson goals
model (time-decayed MLE), blended 45/55, Monte Carlo (20,000 runs) over the
**official** 2026 group draw, then a single-elimination bracket. Group results
use the real draw; knockout pairings are bracket-randomised pending the official
seeding map. Re-runs as live results + news flow in via `python run.py refresh`.*
